reasoning: The Chicago Fed's National Financial Conditions Index (NFCI) exhibits very strong auto-correlation and multi-week structural streaks. After rising monotonically for 10 weeks from -0.507 in late January to -0.383 in mid-April 2025, it has now sharply reversed, decreasing for 4 consecutive weeks to reach -0.421 by May 9, 2025. The downward momentum is solidifying (e.g., a drop of -0.017 in the latest week). Given this streak behavior, there is a low probability that the index will immediately reverse and 'increase' (become less negative) by the first resolution hurdle one week out (May 18). Consequently, the likelihood of an increase is heavily suppressed in the short-term. Over the longer term, the auto-correlation effect weakens, and the index is likely to revert nearer to its historical multi-year average (closer to 0), pushing the 3-month and 6-month probabilities toward 50%.
